schools.summary: Gill: the promise of God being fulfilled, the land subdued, the war at an end, rest had on all sides, and the land divided; he called Reuben, Gad, and half Manasseh.

themes: Then Joshua called the Reubenites, and the Gadites, and the half tribe of Manasseh
God bringing his people's labours to completion and granting rest
there remaineth therefore a rest to the people of God (Heb. 4:9)

schools.summary: Gill: the command was that they should leave their families and flocks in Gilead and pass over Jordan to fight with their brethren; they had obeyed all Joshua commanded.

themes: Ye have kept all that Moses the servant of the LORD commanded you, and have obeyed my voice
faithful service owned and commended by godly leaders
well done, thou good and faithful servant (Matt. 25:21)

schools.summary: Gill: this was for fourteen years — seven in subduing the land, seven in dividing it; they kept the charge of the commandment of the LORD.

themes: Ye have not left your brethren these many days... but have kept the charge of the commandment of the LORD
true brotherly faithfulness persevering to the end
bear ye one another's burdens, and so fulfil the law of Christ (Gal. 6:2)

schools.summary: Gill: the rest given to their brethren as the Lord had promised.
Wesley: though longing for their families, they like good soldiers would not return till dismissed.

themes: the LORD your God hath given rest unto your brethren, as he promised... therefore now return ye, and get you unto your tents
the disciplined awaiting their dismissal, God keeping his promise of rest
the LORD gave them rest round about (Josh. 21:44)

schools.summary: Gill: the commandment and the law — the ten commandments and all other laws, moral, ceremonial, and civil.
Keil & Delitzsch: the charge to do the commandment Moses gave.

themes: to love the LORD your God, and to walk in all his ways... and to cleave unto him, and to serve him with all your heart
the whole of true religion gathered in one charge
thou shalt love the LORD thy God with all thine heart (Deut. 6:5; Matt. 22:37)

schools.summary: Gill: he dismissed them with a blessing, wishing them well and praying God's blessing on them and their families, who had been so useful.

themes: So Joshua blessed them, and sent them away: and they went unto their tents
the faithful dismissed with honour, blessing, and prayer
the LORD bless thee, and keep thee (Num. 6:24)

schools.summary: Gill: Moses had given the one half possession in Bashan, the kingdom of Og.
Keil & Delitzsch: half Manasseh east and half west of Jordan.

themes: to the one half of the tribe of Manasseh Moses had given possession in Bashan: but unto the other half... Joshua
one tribe parted in place yet one people in God
there is one body, and one Spirit... one Lord, one faith (Eph. 4:4-5)

schools.summary: Gill: he spake to the half tribe of Manasseh, though some think to the other tribes also.
Clarke: they returned with their full proportion of the spoils taken from the Canaanites.

themes: Return with much riches unto your tents... divide the spoil of your enemies with your brethren
the law of fair sharing, gains made the portion of all
as his part is that goeth down to the battle, so... that tarrieth by the stuff (1 Sam. 30:24)

schools.summary: Gill: they returned first to the camp to take their substance, then set forward to their own land.
Keil & Delitzsch: them returning to Gilead.

themes: the children of Reuben and... Gad and the half tribe of Manasseh returned... unto the country of Gilead
the homeward journey after warfare, entering their rest
they rest from their labours (Rev. 14:13)

schools.summary: Gill: they came to the banks of Jordan, and built the altar on the Canaan side; it was a great altar to see to.

themes: the children of Reuben and... Gad and the half tribe of Manasseh built there an altar by Jordan, a great altar to see to
a conspicuous memorial of their part in the LORD and his altar
this shall be a sign... when your children ask (Josh. 4:6)

schools.summary: Gill: those who dwelt in Canaan heard, a proof the altar was built on their side; the report spread of the altar over against the land of Canaan.

themes: the children of Israel heard say... have built an altar over against the land of Canaan
a good action misreported and misjudged on partial news
he that answereth a matter before he heareth it, it is folly (Prov. 18:13)

schools.summary: Gill: the nine tribes and a half settled in Canaan heard of the building of the altar.
Keil & Delitzsch: the whole congregation gathering to go to war against them.

themes: the whole congregation of the children of Israel gathered themselves together at Shiloh, to go up to war against them
holy zeal for pure worship, yet grounded on a mistake
they have a zeal of God, but not according to knowledge (Rom. 10:2)

schools.summary: Gill: the children of Israel in the land of Canaan sent the embassy.
Ellicott: Phinehas was well suited to the office, by the constitution of priests and judges Moses established.

themes: the children of Israel sent unto the children of Reuben... Phinehas the son of Eleazar the priest
the wisdom of inquiry before judgment falls
go and tell him his fault between thee and him alone (Matt. 18:15)

schools.summary: Gill: ten princes, of each chief house a prince.
Keil & Delitzsch: each one a head of the house of their fathers among the thousands of Israel.

themes: with him ten princes, of each chief house a prince throughout all the tribes of Israel
matters of worship handled with full and weighty counsel
in the multitude of counsellors there is safety (Prov. 11:14)

schools.summary: Gill: the deputation came to Gilead, either sending for the chiefs or coming to them, and spake with them.

themes: they came unto the children of Reuben... unto the land of Gilead, and they spake with them
dealing openly and directly, hearing before condemning
if thy brother trespass against thee, go and tell him (Matt. 18:15)

schools.summary: Gill: they speak in the name of the congregation of the LORD, not of Israel, because it was a religious, not a civil, matter; they charge them with turning away from following the LORD.

themes: What trespass is this... to turn away this day from following the LORD... that ye might rebel this day against the LORD?
pleading God's cause, jealous for his honour above all
the zeal of thine house hath eaten me up (Ps. 69:9)

schools.summary: Gill: the worshipping of Peor in the plains of Moab — was that sin so small that another must be added? there was a plague in the congregation for it.

themes: Is the iniquity of Peor too little for us, from which we are not cleansed until this day
the remembrance of past judgment restraining from fresh sin
these things... are written for our admonition (1 Cor. 10:11)

schools.summary: Gill: they were not content with the former transgression, but must add this revolt.
Keil & Delitzsch: the fear that God would be wroth with the whole congregation.

themes: seeing ye rebel to day against the LORD... to morrow he will be wroth with the whole congregation of Israel
the solidarity of God's people in the consequences of sin
whether one member suffer, all the members suffer with it (1 Cor. 12:26)

schools.summary: Gill: if their land was judged unclean, having no altar to expiate sin, they were invited over; not to build an altar beside the altar of the LORD.

themes: if the land of your possession be unclean... take possession among us: but rebel not against the LORD
prizing the unity of worship above their own possessions
let us follow after the things which make for peace (Rom. 14:19)

schools.summary: Gill: Achan descended from Zerah the son of Judah.
Keil & Delitzsch: Achan's trespass in the accursed thing, and wrath falling on all the congregation.

themes: Did not Achan the son of Zerah commit a trespass... and wrath fell on all the congregation... that man perished not alone
the dreadful reach of one man's sin among God's people
a little leaven leaveneth the whole lump (1 Cor. 5:6)

schools.summary: Gill: they answered by some person appointed to deliver the answer in their name.
Keil & Delitzsch: the eastern tribes answering the heads of Israel.

themes: Then the children of Reuben and... Gad and the half tribe of Manasseh answered... unto the heads of the thousands of Israel
answering wrongful suspicion with meekness, not resentment
a soft answer turneth away wrath (Prov. 15:1)

schools.summary: Gill: they set out asserting their firm belief that Jehovah, the God of Israel, is the supreme God, God over all; appealed to him who knoweth.

themes: The LORD God of gods, the LORD God of gods, he knoweth, and Israel he shall know
appealing to the all-knowing God with a clear conscience
thou, even thou only, knowest the hearts of all the children of men (1 Kgs 8:39)

schools.summary: Gill: they desire, as they deserve, to be cut off and not saved, if their view in building was to revolt from the pure worship of God; let the LORD himself require it.

themes: if to offer thereon burnt offering... or peace offerings... let the LORD himself require it
an upright heart submitting its cause to God's righteous judgment
judge me, O LORD; for I have walked in mine integrity (Ps. 26:1)

schools.summary: Gill: they were so far from turning to idolatry that it was to guard against everything of the kind for the future; they feared their children might be cut off from the LORD.

themes: for fear of this thing... In time to come your children might speak unto our children... What have ye to do with the LORD God of Israel?
a godly care for the faith of the generations to come
that the generation to come might know them... and declare them to their children (Ps. 78:6)

schools.summary: Gill: they feared it would be represented in time to come that Jordan separated them as a distinct people, and their children made to cease from fearing the LORD.

themes: the LORD hath made Jordan a border... ye have no part in the LORD: so shall your children make our children cease from fearing the LORD
the unity of God's people not to be broken by outward division
there is neither Jew nor Greek... ye are all one in Christ Jesus (Gal. 3:28)

schools.summary: Gill: they said this one to another, to prevent the apostasy of their children and their going into idolatry.
Clarke: an altar not for burnt-offering nor sacrifice, which would have been sin.

themes: Let us now prepare to build us an altar, not for burnt offering, nor for sacrifice
reverencing God's appointed order, avoiding even the appearance of breaking it
in vain do they worship me, teaching for doctrines the commandments of men (Matt. 15:9)

schools.summary: Gill: it was to witness that they are one people, worship one God, and serve at one altar, of which this was a resemblance.
Keil & Delitzsch: the altar as a witness for the generations.

themes: that it may be a witness between us and you, and our generations after us... that we might do the service of the LORD
a memorial of common faith, that the bond be not forgotten
this day shall be unto you for a memorial... throughout your generations (Exod. 12:14)

schools.summary: Gill: they said this to prevent such usage of their children, and that they might have a ready answer.
Ellicott: the words suggest the altar was a pattern of the altar of the LORD.

themes: Behold the pattern of the altar of the LORD, which our fathers made... but it is a witness between us and you
a ready answer left for the faith of the children
be ready always to give an answer... a reason of the hope that is in you (1 Pet. 3:15)

schools.summary: Gill: God ordered one altar, to which all sacrifices were to be brought from every part of Israel; they would not build an altar beside the altar of the LORD before his tabernacle.

themes: God forbid that we should rebel against the LORD... to build an altar... beside the altar of the LORD our God
reverencing the one altar, owning the one sacrifice
by one offering he hath perfected for ever them that are sanctified (Heb. 10:14)

schools.summary: Gill: Phinehas was the son of Eleazar the high priest.
Keil & Delitzsch: Phinehas and the princes hearing the words, and it pleasing them.

themes: when Phinehas the priest, and the princes... heard the words... it pleased them
fair men ready to lay down suspicion on good evidence
charity... rejoiceth not in iniquity, but rejoiceth in the truth (1 Cor. 13:6)

schools.summary: Gill: Phinehas spoke as the mouth of the deputation, in their name.
Keil & Delitzsch: Phinehas declaring the LORD among them.

themes: This day we perceive that the LORD is among us, because ye have not committed this trespass against the LORD
the LORD known to be among his people kept faithful and at peace
if a man love me... we will come unto him, and make our abode with him (John 14:23)

schools.summary: Gill: they were fully satisfied with the relation given, and took their leave of the assembly, and brought word again.

themes: Phinehas... and the princes, returned... unto the land of Canaan, to the children of Israel, and brought them word again
the faithful carrying-back of a true, peace-making report
blessed are the peacemakers: for they shall be called the children of God (Matt. 5:9)

schools.summary: Gill: they were quite satisfied and delighted with what their brethren had done.
Keil & Delitzsch: the thing pleasing Israel.

themes: the children of Israel blessed God, and did not intend to go up against them in battle
the people blessing God for peace preserved and strife averted
behold, how good and how pleasant it is for brethren to dwell together in unity! (Ps. 133:1)

schools.summary: Gill: Ed signifies 'a witness'; they put this word upon it, that its intention might be known in time to come; it shall be a witness that the LORD is God.

themes: the children of Reuben and the children of Gad called the altar Ed: for it shall be a witness between us that the LORD is God
the altar a lasting monument of unity and confession of the one God
the LORD, he is the God; the LORD, he is the God (1 Kgs 18:39)
